none: Environmental Impact Statement, Wacker Drive Extension, U.S. Route 7, Construction

This document details a project to improve air quality within the Illinois Center Development area, specifically addressing carbon monoxide (CO) emissions. The project involves a comprehensive exhaust system designed to enhance the efficiency of CO removal. It’s spearheaded by the Department of Public Works and involves engineers from John Dolio & Associates, Inc. The goal is to evaluate the effectiveness of a system that will reduce CO levels, potentially impacting the development’s overall air quality and public health. The document outlines a cost-benefit analysis comparing the proposed system to typical vehicular tunnel designs, and details the implementation of a catalytic combustion method to minimize CO production. It also includes a conceptual overview of the system, including its components and potential effects of current pollution control laws.
Environmental Impact Statement, Wacker Drive Extension, U.S. Route 7, Construction
